title: Tantor (bounty hunter)

{{Quote|Don't let his slowness of brain or brawn fool you—if Tantor gets his hands/hooves on you, it's over.|General Airen Cracken|<ref name="WC" />}}
'''Tantor''' was an [[Esoomian/Legends|Esoomian]] born on [[Esooma]] in [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]]. He worked as a [[Pirate/Legends|pirate]] brawler and stopped several mutiny attempts that threatened [[Unidentified pirate captain (Tantor)|his captain]]. After the [[Captain/Legends|captain]] and Tantor argued about a raise in Tantor's salary, the Esoomian attacked and jettisoned the captain into [[Realspace/Legends|space]]. Tantor was shackled following the incident, for the crew's safety, but later freed himself and [[Death/Legends|killed]] several of the pirates before escaping.

Through the following [[Standard year/Legends|years]], Tantor became a professional enforcer, [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]], [[Bodyguard/Legends|bodyguard]], thug, and [[Assassin/Legends|killer]]-for-hire. Lacking the slyness needed for the most profitable jobs in his area, and knowing that he was not that smart, Tantor carefully chose less lucrative, easier tasks. During the first years of the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]], he operated in [[Mos Eisley/Legends|Mos Eisley]], confronting [[Gamorrean/Legends|Gamorreans]] and agents of the New Republic—which earned him a [[Bounty/Legends|bounty]] on his head. His employer, the [[Enu]] [[Crime lord/Legends|crime lord]] [[Corf Sarb]], had initially agreed to pay Tantor's medical bills, but he later changed his mind. Knowing this, Tantor then turned against Sarb.

==Biography==
===Asking a pirate for a payrise===
[[File:Tantor_GG10.jpg|thumb|left|200px|Tantor]]
An [[Esoomian/Legends|Esoomian]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] born on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Esooma]]<ref name="WC" /> in [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]],<ref name="WC Dates" /> Tantor first left his [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] to join the crew of a [[Pirate/Legends|pirate]] [[Starship/Legends|starship]]. Tantor's tasks included intimidating victims of the ships they boarded. The Esoomian also subjugated the pirate crew during several attempted mutinies, to the benefit of [[Unidentified pirate captain (Tantor)|the vessel's captain]].<ref name="GG">''[[Galaxy Guide 10: Bounty Hunters]]''</ref>

After a long [[Time/Legends|time]] without a pay rise, Tantor requested one of the [[Captain/Legends|captain]]. As Tantor and the captain had differing opinions on the pay adjustment, a discussion followed, which ended with an unsatisfied Tantor putting the captain in an [[Escape pod/Legends|escape pod]] and jettisoning the individual into [[Realspace/Legends|space]]. The captain was succeeded by [[Unidentified pirate first officer|the ship's first officer]], who mistrusted Tantor after this event. As such, the [[Executive officer/Legends|first officer]] decided to shackle the Esoomian for the crew's safety. Tantor freed himself at the following stopover, [[Death/Legends|killed]] a number of pirates, and escaped the ship.<ref name="GG" />

===Small-time bounty hunter===
Eventually obtaining an [[Imperial Peace-Keeping Certificate/Legends|Imperial Peace-Keeping Certificate]], Tantor became a legitimate [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]], albeit an aimless one. Realizing that his mind was not as keen as some of his colleagues', Tantor mostly worked for other individuals while sometimes accepting illegal jobs in exchange for [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|money]] or favors. He nonetheless avoided the higher [[Bounty/Legends|bounties]], as they meant a harder job, and the Esoomian contented himself with a modest success. Still, he was familiar with the reputation of [[Jabba Desilijic Tiure/Legends|Jabba]] the [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]], a [[Crime lord/Legends|crime lord]] operating on [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]], and Tantor hoped to travel there and join [[Jabba's criminal empire/Legends|Jabba's organization]] one day.<ref name="GG" />
[[File:Enu.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Tantor worked for crime lord Corf Sarb.]]
However, by the time Tantor reached Tatooine, Jabba had already [[Battle of the Great Pit of Carkoon|been killed]], and Tantor no longer had the Peace Keeping Certificate identifying him as an authorized bounty hunter. He looked for alternative employers, including the [[Enu]] [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggler]] and crime lord [[Corf Sarb]], for whom he began working. Sarb was known to hire the best available thugs and to have them affirm their loyalty to him by killing one of his enemies or committing a crime. While working for Sarb and for other crime bosses, Tantor earned himself a reputation in [[Mos Eisley/Legends|Mos Eisley]] as an "antennae-breaker."<ref name="WC" />

At some point before [[7 ABY/Legends|7 ABY]],<ref name="WC Dates" /> Sarb promised to pay for Tantor's medical expenses should Tantor fight against a clan of [[Gamorrean/Legends|Gamorreans]]. However, once the skirmish was over, Sarb broke his oath. Tantor's partnership with Sarb soured, and the Esoomian demolished Sarb's headquarters while the Enu was inside. Tantor also fought and assaulted agents of the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]]. Due to this, [[New Republic Intelligence Service|New Republic Intelligence]] offered a bounty of 30,000 credits for Tantor's head.<ref name="WC" />

==Personality and traits==
Tantor worked as a professional enforcer, [[Bodyguard/Legends|bodyguard]], and [[Assassin/Legends|assassin]]-for-hire, sometimes collecting debts for anyone paying him. The Esoomian commonly resorted to physical violence in a straightforward style, effective in the back alleys he frequented.<ref name="GG" /> While he was not particularly intelligent, he had a certain guile and ambushed prey from dark alleys. A report on Tantor written by agents of New Republic Intelligence mentioned that Tantor was surprisingly loyal for a common thug. Nonetheless, Tantor disliked being betrayed by his boss, Corf Sarb, and turned against him.<ref name="WC" />

At three [[Meter/Legends|meters]] tall, Tantor was huge and strong. He knew how to use his natural heftiness to his advantage, both in personal combat and as a tool of intimidation. He was very persuasive and had a penchant for [[Gambling/Legends|gambling]].<ref name="GG" /> and he could also use his naked, hooved fists to violently kill a person. Like many Esoomians, he had trouble speaking [[Galactic Basic Standard/Legends|Galactic Basic Standard]] and spoke in a garbled tone, but understood it well enough to follow orders.<ref name="WC" />

==Equipment==
Tantor carried a [[Vibro-ax/Legends|vibro-ax]] and wore a black [[Pauldron/Legends|pauldron]] on each shoulder, a [[Belt/Legends|belt]], and black [[Clothing/Legends|pants]].<ref name="GG" />

==Behind the scenes==
Tantor first appeared in [[Wanted by Cracken (sourcebook)|''Wanted by Cracken'']], a [[1993]] sourcebook for the [[West End Games]]' ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]''. The book included an image of Tantor, credited to [[Lucasfilm]], which was a cropped, re-inked<ref name="WC" /> conceptual design for the [[Rancor/Legends|rancor]], drawn by [[Joe Johnston]] for the [[1983]] film [[Star Wars: Episode VI Return of the Jedi|''Star Wars'': Episode VI ''Return of the Jedi'']] but went unused.<ref name="sketch">''[[Return of the Jedi Sketchbook]]''</ref> Tantor later appeared in the [[1994]] [[roleplaying game]] supplement, ''[[Galaxy Guide 10: Bounty Hunters]]'', which included the first piece of original art on Tantor, drawn by [[Mike Vilardi]].<ref name="GG" />
